name: Bordeaux to Lacanau-Océan Greenway
name_original: Balade entre les pins jusqu’à Lacanau
description: This moderate 79.3-mile road cycling route takes you through pine forests and past Lacanau Lake, with 952 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: No traffic road cycling routes around the Garonne-Kanal offer a predominantly flat and well-maintained cycling experience through southwestern France. The region is characterized by its extensive network of dedicated cycling paths, often following the historic towpaths of the Garonne Canal, which runs parallel to the Garonne River. Cyclists traverse diverse landscapes including agricultural fields, vineyards, and tree-lined sections providing natural shade, connecting urban centers like Bordeaux and Toulouse. The terrain is generally low-elevation, with some routes featuring gentle hills, making it accessible for various fitness levels. Best no traffic road cycling routes around the Garonne-Kanal The most popular no traffic road cycling route is Bordeaux to Lacanau-Océan Greenway, a 79.3 miles (127.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 55 minutes to complete. This route offers a journey from the city towards the Atlantic, passing through pine forests and near lakes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Walk on the Roger-Lapébie cycle path, a moderate 68.4 miles (110.1 km) path. This greenway is known for its flat, dedicated surface, winding through rural landscapes and charming villages. Local road cyclists also love the Hilly loop in the vineyards, a 34.6 miles (55.7 km) trail leading through renowned wine regions, often completed in about 2 hours 43 minutes. Road cycling around the Garonne-Kanal is defined by flat canal-side paths, extensive vineyards, and agricultural fields, with dedicated greenways ensuring a safe experience away from vehicle traffic. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy, flat sections to routes with moderate climbs. The routes in Garonne canal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1,700 reviews. More than 40,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Garonne canal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no traffic road cycling routes are available around the Garonne Canal?
A: There are over 500 dedicated no traffic road cycling routes around the Garonne Canal listed on komoot. These routes offer a fantastic way to explore the region safely and serenely, away from vehicle traffic.

Q: What makes the Garonne Canal ideal for no traffic road cycling?
A: The Garonne Canal is exceptional for no traffic road cycling due to its flat, well-maintained, and often shaded paths. Much of the route consists of dedicated cycling paths, ensuring a safe experience. The ubiquitous plane trees provide welcome shade, especially during warmer months, and the paths are mostly asphalted, making for easy and comfortable riding.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or families with children?
A: Yes, the Garonne Canal is highly suitable for cyclists of all levels, including beginners and families. The paths are generally flat and dedicated, making them safe and accessible. Komoot offers over 110 easy routes that are perfect for a relaxed ride with children or those new to road cycling.

Q: When is the best time of year to cycle the Garonne Canal?
A: The Garonne Canal is enjoyable for cycling throughout much of the year. However, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. During warmer months, the abundant tree-lined paths provide welcome shade, making it comfortable even on sunnier days.

Q: Can I find circular no traffic road cycling routes along the Garonne Canal?
A: While the main canal path is linear, many routes in the surrounding region offer circular options that incorporate sections of the no traffic paths. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e825369415/roger-lapebie-and-entre-deux-mers-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roger Lapébie and Entre-deux-Mers Loop&lt;/a&gt; provides a varied ride through vineyards and charming countryside.

Q: What historical landmarks can I discover along the no traffic routes?
A: The Garonne Canal region is rich in history. You can encounter impressive structures like the Agen aqueduct, which carries the canal over the Garonne River. Near Bordeaux, you might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1025678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château du Hâ&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5191084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sainte-Croix-du-Mont&lt;/a&gt; historical site. Further along, charming medieval towns and bastides like Moissac with its UNESCO World Heritage-listed abbey are easily accessible from the canal paths.

Q: Are there any natural features or scenic viewpoints to enjoy?
A: Absolutely. The routes often run alongside the Garonne River, offering picturesque views. You'll cycle through diverse agricultural landscapes, including fields of corn, wheat, and sunflowers, and traverse renowned wine regions with vineyards. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/929964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bordeaux Water Mirror&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/985257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Girondins&lt;/a&gt; are notable man-made monuments, while natural lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3302745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Major of Colomers&lt;/a&gt; offer serene stops.

Q: Is public transport accessible for reaching these no traffic routes?
A: Yes, the Garonne Canal is well-connected by public transport, especially at its major urban centers. Bordeaux and Toulouse serve as excellent starting or ending points for longer tours and are easily reachable by train. Many smaller towns along the canal also have train stations, allowing for flexible access to various sections of the cycling path.

Q: Where can I find parking for these no traffic road cycling routes?
A: Parking is generally available in the towns and villages along the Garonne Canal. Major cities like Bordeaux and Toulouse offer numerous parking facilities. For specific starting points, it's advisable to check local parking options in smaller towns such as Agen, Moissac, or La Réole, which often have designated parking areas near the canal.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay along the Garonne Canal cycling paths?
A: Yes, the Garonne Canal route passes through numerous towns and villages where you can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. From bustling city centers like Bordeaux and Toulouse to charming smaller towns like Agen and Moissac, there are plenty of opportunities to refuel and rest. Many establishments cater specifically to cyclists.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the no traffic routes around the Garonne Canal?
A: The no traffic road cycling routes around the Garonne Canal are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1700 ratings. Reviewers often praise the peaceful, car-free environment, the flat and well-maintained surfaces, and the beautiful, shaded scenery. The accessibility for all skill levels and the blend of natural beauty with historical towns are frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any challenging no traffic road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: While many canal paths are flat, the surrounding region offers more challenging options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e843167390/sequence-of-climbs-in-entre-deux-mers" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sequence of climbs in Entre-Deux-Mers&lt;/a&gt; provides a difficult route with significant elevation gain, perfect for experienced road cyclists looking for a workout away from traffic. Komoot lists over 50 difficult routes in the area.

Q: Can I combine cycling with exploring vineyards in the region?
A: Absolutely! The Garonne Canal traverses renowned wine regions like Bordeaux and Entre-deux-Mers. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e826309711/hilly-loop-in-the-vineyards" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hilly loop in the vineyards&lt;/a&gt;, take you directly through picturesque vineyards, offering opportunities to explore local wineries and sample regional wines. It's a perfect way to combine your passion for cycling with wine tasting.
tips: It is worth taking a short break here and enjoying the view.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the 'Walk among the pines to Lacanau' route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features paved and smooth surfaces, making it ideal for road bikes. While generally flat, you might encounter gentle ascents and descents, especially in sections that traverse dune areas. It largely uses dedicated cycle paths, often converted forest roads, ensuring a ride mostly free from car traffic.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, many of the cycling routes in the Lacanau area, including this one, are considered easy to moderate. The overall flat topography and dedicated car-free paths make it accessible for a wide range of cyclists, including families and those new to road cycling.

faqs.question: What natural features or highlights will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll be immersed in the natural beauty of the region, primarily cycling through extensive pine forests. The route also offers views of the large freshwater Lacanau Lake and is in proximity to the Atlantic Ocean and its beaches. You'll also pass by the Greenway around Saumos and the Boulevard de la Plage Promenade.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to ride this route?
faqs.answer: The route is enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds. Summer is also popular, but the pine forests provide welcome shade. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Is the 'Walk among the pines to Lacanau' route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail research does not specifically mention dog-friendly rules for this cycling route. While dogs are generally allowed on many outdoor paths, it's best to check local regulations regarding leashes and waste disposal, especially on dedicated cycle paths or in protected natural areas.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access this cycling route. It utilizes public cycle paths and forest roads.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the start of the route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking locations for this exact route aren't detailed, the Lacanau area offers various public parking options near its extensive network of cycle paths. Consider starting near the Salaunes Town Center or Lacanau Lake for convenient access.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like water or repair facilities along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, some sections of the broader cycling network around Lacanau, which this route is part of, feature rest areas that may include water and repair facilities. It's always a good idea to carry your own water and basic repair kit.

faqs.question: Does this route connect to other cycling paths or long-distance trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, a significant portion of this route is integrated into the Vélodyssée (EuroVelo 1), a well-known long-distance European cycle route. It also includes sections of the Voie Verte Bordeaux - Lacanau Océan and the Piste Cyclable Bordeaux - Lacanau, offering connections to other towns and attractions.

faqs.question: Is this a car-free route?
faqs.answer: A major highlight of the cycling network around Lacanau, including this route, is the extensive network of dedicated cycle paths. This provides a safe and peaceful cycling experience largely away from vehicular traffic.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is best suited for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the predominantly paved and smooth surfaces, a road bike is highly suitable for this route. Its design is optimized for efficiency on such terrain, aligning with the 'racebike' sport type indicated for the tour.
